name: augment_code_search
description:
Augment Code Search: Search for code in a GitHub repository using natural language queries.

This tool retrieves relevant code snippets from indexed GitHub repositories. Use it when you have a conceptual question but don't know the exact file or symbol name.

For exact keyword or phrase searches, prefer grep or other text search tools.

To get repo_owner and repo_name from git: git remote get-url origin

Examples of good queries:
- "function that handles user authentication"
- "database connection setup code"
- "tests for the payment processing module"
- "error handling for API requests"

Examples of bad queries:
- "foo_bar" (use grep for exact symbol search)
- "TODO" (use grep for keyword search)
- "code that deals with everything" (too vague)

If the repository is not found or not indexed, the error will suggest similar available repositories.

repo_owner.description: The GitHub organization or username that owns the repository. For example, for github.com/augmentcode/test-repo, use 'augmentcode'.

repo_name.description: The repository name without the owner prefix. For example, for github.com/augmentcode/test-repo, use 'test-repo'.

query.description: The natural language query describing what code you're looking for. For example: 'function that handles user authentication' or 'database connection setup code'.

branch.description: The branch name to search. Defaults to 'main'. Do not include 'refs/heads/' prefix.

max_results.description: Maximum number of code snippets to return. Defaults to 5.
